justification: As the operator, A is sharper and more useful. A delivers a structured 50-review sample with a rating-distribution chart, language split (66% PL / 34% EN), Local-Guide share (36%), and three named complaint patterns with concrete percentages — Biang Biang execution polarity (33% of negatives), return-visit quality drop (8%), and defensive management responses (6%). Each maps directly to a Monday-morning intervention. B reframes the authenticity critique as 'isolated' and rests its vulnerability case on a single stale-soup anecdote, which softens rather than sharpens the picture an owner actually needs. Both dispatched the right pair (review_analyst + guest_intelligence) and both pull from Trojmiasto, delivery apps, and Google, so source diversity and specialist correctness tie. A's rating-math (215/50 = 4.30 vs. stated 4.34) is a minor drift but its claims are internally cross-referenced; B is internally consistent but thinner. A wins overall on coverage, specificity, and actionability.
